# Plan 003: Keep the autogenerated registry `Index` out of the client bundle

> **Executor instructions**: Follow this plan step by step. Run every
> verification command and confirm the expected result before moving to the
> next step. If anything in the "STOP conditions" section occurs, stop and
> report — do not improvise. When done, update the status row for this plan
> in `plans/README.md`.
>
> **Drift check (run first)**: `git diff --stat 557eaf4f..HEAD -- src/components/component-preview.tsx src/components/mdx.tsx src/registry/__index__.tsx`
> If any of these changed since this plan was written, compare the "Current
> state" excerpts against the live code before proceeding; on a mismatch, treat
> it as a STOP condition.

## Status

- **Priority**: P2
- **Effort**: M
- **Risk**: MED
- **Depends on**: 002 recommended (so you can rely on a test/verify loop), not required
- **Category**: perf
- **Planned at**: commit `557eaf4f`, 2026-06-14

## Why this matters

`src/components/component-preview.tsx` is a Client Component (`"use client"`)
that statically imports the entire autogenerated registry index:
`import { Index } from "@/registry/__index__"`. That file is ~1600 lines and
contains, for every registry item (~150 of them), its metadata (name,
description, file paths, targets) plus a `React.lazy(() => import(...))` thunk.
Because `ComponentPreview` is mapped into MDX rendering
(`src/components/mdx.tsx`), every documentation/blog page that renders a
`<ComponentPreview>` pulls this whole map into the client module graph — even
though each render only needs the single component named by the `name` prop.

The goal: a `<ComponentPreview name="x">` should only cause the client to load
component `x`, not the index of all components. This is a measure-first plan: you
will confirm the bundle impact before and after, and STOP if the impact turns out
negligible (so we don't take MED risk for no gain).

## Current state

`src/components/component-preview.tsx` (lines 1–3, 24, 53–65):

```tsx
"use client"

import React, { useMemo, useState } from "react"

// ...
import { Index } from "@/registry/__index__"

export function ComponentPreview({
  className,
  name,
  /* ... */ children,
  ...props
}) {
  const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme()
  const [replay, setReplay] = useState(0)
  // ...
  const Preview = useMemo(() => {
    const Component = Index[name]?.component
    if (!Component) {
      return (
        <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
          Component <CodeInline>{name}</CodeInline> not found in registry.
        </p>
      )
    }
    return <Component />
  }, [name])
  // ... renders Tabs with a Suspense-wrapped {Preview}, replay button, customize/v0 buttons
}
```

`src/registry/__index__.tsx` (autogenerated — DO NOT hand-edit; header says so):

```tsx
export const Index: Record<string, any> = {
  haptic: {
    name: "haptic",
    description: "...",
    type: "registry:lib",
    files: [{ path: "...", type: "...", target: "..." }],
    component: React.lazy(async () => {
      const mod = await import("@/registry/lib/haptic/haptic.ts")
      // ...
      return { default: mod.default || mod[exportName] }
    }),
    categories: ["utilities"],
    meta: undefined,
  },
  // ... ~150 more entries
}
```

`src/components/mdx.tsx` (lines 43, 64) maps the component for MDX:

```tsx
import { ComponentPreview } from "./component-preview"

// ...
const components = { /* ... */ ComponentPreview /* ... */ }
```

Server-side callers that ALSO use `Index` and are already fine (they use it on
the server — DO NOT change them): `src/lib/blocks.ts` (`await import(...)`),
`src/app/(preview)/preview/[name]/page.tsx`, `src/app/(rss)/blocks/rss/route.ts`.

Convention note: `src/registry/__index__.tsx` is autogenerated by
`pnpm registry:build`. NEVER edit it by hand; if its shape must change, change the
generator `src/scripts/build-registry.mts` and rebuild. This plan does NOT change
the generator — it changes how `ComponentPreview` consumes the existing `Index`.

## Commands you will need

| Purpose    | Command            | Expected on success               |
| ---------- | ------------------ | --------------------------------- |
| Install    | `pnpm install`     | exit 0                            |
| Build      | `pnpm build`       | exit 0; prints route bundle sizes |
| Typecheck  | `pnpm check-types` | exit 0                            |
| Lint       | `pnpm lint`        | exit 0                            |
| Dev server | `pnpm dev`         | serves at https://ncdai.localhost |

## Step 0 (MEASURE FIRST — gate the whole plan)

Run a production build and record the First Load JS / per-route sizes for a doc
route that renders `<ComponentPreview>` (e.g. a `/components/<slug>` page).

```bash
pnpm build 2>&1 | tee /tmp/build-before.txt
```

Save `/tmp/build-before.txt`. Note the First Load JS of the components doc route
and of the shared chunks. This is your baseline. If you cannot identify any route
whose bundle plausibly includes `__index__` (e.g. the build output shows the
component-doc routes are server-rendered with negligible client JS), STOP and
report — the premise may not hold for this build and the refactor's risk is not
justified.

## Scope

**In scope** (the only files you should modify/create):

- `src/components/component-preview.tsx`
- `src/components/component-preview-shell.tsx` (create — the client interactivity shell)

**Out of scope** (do NOT touch):

- `src/registry/__index__.tsx` and `src/registry/registry.autogenerated.json`,
  `public/r/*.json` — autogenerated; never hand-edit.
- `src/scripts/build-registry.mts` — changing the generator is a separate, larger
  effort; this plan works with the index as generated.
- `src/lib/blocks.ts`, `src/app/(preview)/preview/[name]/page.tsx`,
  `src/app/(rss)/blocks/rss/route.ts` — they use `Index` server-side correctly.
- `src/components/mdx.tsx` — the MDX mapping stays pointing at `ComponentPreview`;
  only `ComponentPreview`'s internals change.

## Git workflow

- Branch: `advisor/003-registry-index-client-bundle`
- Commit message: `perf: resolve registry component server-side to shrink client bundle`
- Do NOT push or open a PR unless the operator instructed it.

## Steps

### Step 1: Split ComponentPreview into a server resolver + client shell

Goal: move the `Index[name]` lookup to a Server Component so the `__index__`
import never enters the client graph. The interactive parts (tabs, replay button,
theme remount, customize/v0 buttons) move into a new client shell that receives
the already-resolved preview element as a prop.

1. Create `src/components/component-preview-shell.tsx` as `"use client"`. Move the
   current interactive JSX (the `Tabs`, replay `useState`, `useTheme`-based
   remount keying, customize/v0/replay buttons, the `Suspense` wrapper, and the
   `code` tab) here. Its props are the resolved preview node and the same display
   flags. Suggested signature:

   ```tsx
   "use client"
   export function ComponentPreviewShell({
     preview,
     code,
     canReplay,
     customizeUrl,
     openInV0Url,
     remountOnThemeChange,
     prose,
     className,
     ...props
   }: {
     preview: React.ReactNode
     code: React.ReactNode /* + the existing flags */
   } & React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
     /* moved JSX */
   }
   ```

   Keep the existing remount behaviour: the `key` that currently combines
   `replay` and `resolvedTheme` stays here (this shell owns `replay` state and
   `useTheme`).

2. Rewrite `src/components/component-preview.tsx` as a **Server Component**
   (remove `"use client"`). It keeps the same exported name and props
   (`name`, `openInV0Url`, `customizeUrl`, `canReplay`, `prose`,
   `codeCollapsible`, `remountOnThemeChange`, `children`). It does the
   `Index[name]` lookup, builds the preview element (`<Component />` or the
   "not found" message), splits `children` into the code element, and returns
   `<ComponentPreviewShell preview={...} code={...} {...flags} />`.
   The `React.lazy` component still works rendered from the server into a Client
   Component subtree via the shell's `Suspense` boundary — the lazy `import()`
   resolves on the client, which is the desired "load only this component"
   behaviour.

Reference: the current file already wraps `{Preview}` in `<React.Suspense>` —
preserve that boundary inside the shell.

**Verify**: `pnpm check-types` → exit 0; `pnpm lint` → exit 0.

### Step 2: Confirm `Index` no longer imports into the client

```bash
grep -n "registry/__index__" src/components/component-preview.tsx
grep -n "use client" src/components/component-preview.tsx
```

**Verify**: `component-preview.tsx` still imports `Index` (it is now server) but
no longer starts with `"use client"`; `component-preview-shell.tsx` does NOT
import `@/registry/__index__`.

### Step 3: Rebuild and compare bundle sizes

```bash
pnpm build 2>&1 | tee /tmp/build-after.txt
```

Compare the components doc route First Load JS in `/tmp/build-after.txt` against
`/tmp/build-before.txt` from Step 0.

**Verify**: the components doc route's First Load JS is smaller (or the large
shared registry chunk no longer loads on it). If sizes are unchanged, STOP and
report — the refactor did not achieve its goal and should be reviewed before
merging.

### Step 4: Manually verify preview still works

Run `pnpm dev`, open a component doc page (a `/components/<slug>` route that uses
`<ComponentPreview>`), and confirm:

- the Preview tab renders the live component,
- the Code tab shows the code block,
- the Replay button (where present) re-mounts the component,
- theme switching still re-mounts components that set `remountOnThemeChange`,
- an unknown name still shows the "not found in registry" message.

**Verify**: all five behaviours work; no console errors about lazy/Suspense.

## Test plan

- No new unit tests (this is a rendering/bundling refactor; the behaviour is
  visual). Verification is the build-size diff (Step 3) and the manual checks
  (Step 4).
- If plan 002 has landed, you may add a lightweight test asserting
  `component-preview-shell.tsx` does not import `@/registry/__index__` (a source
  grep test), to prevent regression. Optional.

## Done criteria

ALL must hold:

- [ ] `component-preview.tsx` no longer has `"use client"`; the new shell does
- [ ] `grep "registry/__index__" src/components/component-preview-shell.tsx` → no match
- [ ] `pnpm check-types` and `pnpm lint` exit 0
- [ ] `pnpm build` exits 0 and the target route's First Load JS is smaller than baseline (Step 3)
- [ ] Manual checks in Step 4 all pass
- [ ] Only the two in-scope files changed (`git status`)
- [ ] `plans/README.md` status row for 003 updated to DONE

## STOP conditions

Stop and report back (do not improvise) if:

- Step 0 or Step 3 shows the bundle impact is negligible — the MED risk is not
  worth it; report the numbers.
- Splitting breaks the lazy/Suspense rendering (component renders blank, hydration
  error, or "Cannot read properties of undefined") and a reasonable fix attempt
  fails twice.
- The refactor appears to require editing `src/registry/__index__.tsx` or the
  generator `build-registry.mts` — that means a different approach; report.
- Passing the `<Component />` element from server to the client shell triggers a
  serialization error ("Functions cannot be passed to Client Components") — report;
  the element-as-children approach should avoid this, but if it doesn't, the shape
  needs rethinking.

## Maintenance notes

- This pattern (resolve registry items server-side, hand the element to a thin
  client shell) is the model for any other Client Component that currently imports
  `__index__`. The preview iframe route already imports `Index` server-side.
- If the generator (`build-registry.mts`) is ever changed to emit a separate
  lightweight name→loader map, this resolver can switch to it.
- Reviewer should scrutinize: that no interactive behaviour regressed (replay,
  theme remount), and the before/after bundle numbers in the PR description.
